info.name: Task Management System — Full API Collection
info.description:
Complete API test collection for the Task Management System.

HOW JWT WORKS IN THIS COLLECTION:
Every endpoint requires a valid Keycloak JWT. Run a Login request first — the test script stores the token in {{access_token}}. The collection-level auth (Bearer {{access_token}}) automatically sends it on every subsequent request.

SETUP:
1. docker compose up -d --build
2. Wait ~60s for Keycloak to start
3. Import this collection into Postman
4. Run any Login request first
5. Run any CRUD request — token is sent automatically

COLLECTION VARIABLES:
base_url        = http://localhost:8080
keycloak_url    = http://localhost:8180
realm           = internship-task-realm
client_id       = task-frontend
access_token    = (populated by login requests)
task_id         = (populated by create/get requests)

item.name: Auth — Keycloak Tokens

exec: console.log('Logged in as admin1 (ADMIN). Token expires in', json.expires_in, 's');

urlencoded.description: openid scope required for preferred_username claim in JWT

request.description:
Gets a JWT for admin1 (ADMIN role). The test script stores it in {{access_token}}.

Decoded JWT contains:
  preferred_username: 'admin1'
  realm_access.roles: ['ADMIN']

Spring Boot's KeycloakJwtAuthConverter reads realm_access.roles and creates ROLE_ADMIN authority.

exec: console.log('Logged in as user1 (USER). Can only modify own tasks.');
console.log('Logged in as user2 (USER). Used for ownership restriction tests.');
console.log('Token refreshed. New token expires in', json.expires_in, 's');

request.description: Gets a new access_token using the refresh_token. Use this if the access token expired (after 5 minutes).

exec: console.log('PASS: Keycloak correctly rejected bad credentials');

item.description:
Login requests for each demo user. Run one first to populate {{access_token}}.

Uses Keycloak Resource Owner Password Credentials (ROPC) grant — Postman-friendly direct login without browser redirects.

item.name: Tasks — GET (Read)

request.description: Returns all tasks. Authorization: Bearer {{access_token}} is sent automatically by the collection-level auth.

item.name: FAIL — GET all tasks, no token → 401

request.description: Overrides collection-level auth with 'No Auth'. Spring Security returns 401 before the controller runs.

item.description: Read operations query MongoDB directly — no Kafka involved. Both ADMIN and USER roles can read all tasks.

item.name: Tasks — POST (Create)

exec: console.log('Wait 1-2s then run GET to confirm task is in MongoDB');

body.raw:
{
  "title": "Prepare internship report",
  "description": "Complete the weekly internship summary and submit by Friday",
  "status": "PENDING",
  "priority": "HIGH",
  "dueDate": "2027-06-30"
}

request.description: createdBy is NOT in the request body. Spring Boot extracts it from JWT preferred_username automatically and sets it on the saved Task.

body.raw:
{
  "title": "Review pull request",
  "description": "Review the feature branch changes before merging to main",
  "status": "PENDING",
  "priority": "MEDIUM",
  "dueDate": "2027-08-15"
}

request.description: Triggers @NotBlank on title in TaskRequestDTO. GlobalExceptionHandler catches WebExchangeBindException → 400.

item.description: Returns 202 Accepted (NOT 201) — task is saved async via Kafka. Wait 1-2 seconds after 202 before running GET to verify the task exists in MongoDB.

item.name: Tasks — PUT (Full Update)

body.raw:
{
  "title": "Prepare internship report (UPDATED)",
  "description": "Updated: include code review feedback and mentor comments",
  "status": "PENDING",
  "priority": "HIGH",
  "dueDate": "2027-07-30"
}

item.name: FAIL — PUT wrong user (user2 on user1 task) → 403

request.description:
SETUP: 1) Login as USER1  2) Create task  3) Login as USER2  4) Run this.

validateOwnership(): getCurrentUsername()='user2', task.createdBy='user1', isAdmin=false → AccessDeniedException → 403.

item.description: Full update — replaces all fields. Only works when task is PENDING. Ownership check runs BEFORE Kafka: validateOwnership() is called synchronously while the JWT SecurityContext is active. 403 is returned immediately if ownership fails — no Kafka event is published.

item.name: Tasks — PATCH (Status Update)

request.description: TaskController publishes TaskUpdatedEvent with null title. TaskServiceImpl sees null title → MODE A (status-only update).
SETUP: Login as USER2 while {{task_id}} belongs to USER1.

exec: pm.test('Status 409 Conflict (if task is still PENDING)', () => {

request.description: SETUP: Make sure {{task_id}} is a PENDING task. validateStatusTransition() blocks PENDING→COMPLETED. Must pass through IN_PROGRESS.

item.description:
Status-only update. Transition rules enforced by validateStatusTransition():
  PENDING → IN_PROGRESS  ALLOWED
  IN_PROGRESS → COMPLETED  ALLOWED
  PENDING → COMPLETED  BLOCKED (must go through IN_PROGRESS)
  COMPLETED → anything  BLOCKED (final state)

item.name: Tasks — DELETE

exec: pm.test('Deletion event confirmed', () => pm.expect(pm.response.text()).to.include('Task deletion event published'));
console.log('Wait 1-2s then run GET by ID to confirm it returns 404');

item.name: FAIL — DELETE wrong user (user2 on user1 task) → 403

request.description:
SETUP: 1) Login as USER1  2) Create task  3) Login as USER2  4) Run this.

validateOwnership() → AccessDeniedException → GlobalExceptionHandler.handleForbidden() → 403 with ErrorResponse body.

item.description: Ownership checked before Kafka event is published. COMPLETED tasks can be deleted.

item.name: E2E — ADMIN Full Lifecycle

exec: const j=pm.response.json(); if(j.access_token){pm.collectionVariables.set('access_token',j.access_token); console.log('E2E Step 1: logged in as admin1');} pm.test('200 OK',()=>pm.response.to.have.status(200));

pm.test('200 OK — in MongoDB',()=>pm.response.to.have.status(200)); pm.test('createdBy is admin1',()=>pm.expect(pm.response.json().createdBy).to.equal('admin1')); pm.test('status is PENDING',()=>pm.expect(pm.response.json().status).to.equal('PENDING')); console.log('E2E Step 3: confirmed in MongoDB');
pm.test('202 Accepted',()=>pm.response.to.have.status(202)); console.log('E2E Step 4: update event published');
pm.test('202 Accepted',()=>pm.response.to.have.status(202)); console.log('E2E Step 7: delete event published');
pm.test('404 Not Found — deleted from MongoDB',()=>pm.response.to.have.status(404)); console.log('E2E COMPLETE: full lifecycle verified');

item.description: Run IN ORDER as a complete smoke test. 8 steps: login → create → verify → update → advance to IN_PROGRESS → advance to COMPLETED → delete → verify gone.

item.name: E2E — Ownership Restriction (USER1 vs USER2 vs ADMIN)

body.raw:
{
  "title": "USER1 ownership test task",
  "description": "Only user1 and ADMIN can modify this",
  "status": "PENDING",
  "priority": "MEDIUM",
  "dueDate": "2027-12-31"
}

item.name: Own 4 — USER2 tries PUT on USER1 task → 403

exec: pm.test('403 Forbidden',()=>pm.response.to.have.status(403)); pm.test('Error says no permission',()=>pm.expect(pm.response.json().message).to.include('permission')); console.log('PASS: USER2 blocked from modifying USER1 task');

body.raw:
{
  "title": "Hacked by user2",
  "description": "This should be blocked by validateOwnership()",
  "status": "PENDING",
  "priority": "LOW",
  "dueDate": "2027-12-31"
}

item.name: Own 5 — USER2 tries DELETE on USER1 task → 403

exec: pm.test('403 Forbidden',()=>pm.response.to.have.status(403)); console.log('PASS: USER2 blocked from deleting USER1 task');

item.name: Own 7 — ADMIN updates USER1 task → 202 (ADMIN bypasses ownership)

exec: pm.test('202 Accepted — ADMIN overrides ownership',()=>pm.response.to.have.status(202)); console.log('PASS: Ownership scenario complete. USER2=403, ADMIN=202');

body.raw:
{
  "title": "ADMIN updated USER1 task",
  "description": "ADMIN has full access regardless of createdBy",
  "status": "PENDING",
  "priority": "HIGH",
  "dueDate": "2027-12-31"
}

item.description:
Run IN ORDER to prove the ownership enforcement works end-to-end:

1. USER1 logs in and creates task
    
2. USER2 tries PUT → 403 blocked
    
3. USER2 tries DELETE → 403 blocked
    
4. ADMIN overrides and updates successfully → 202
